observations.citation: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL), 1997-06-04 (0EB4F7B76F4FACD0): absentees counted into predawn; 'won't start counting about 4,500 provisional ballots until 9 a.m. today' - conservative night floor; stadium-election all-nighter

observations.label: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL)
observations.citation: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL), 1996-03-28 (0EB4F73F7318D872): 'With all but a few provisional or disputed ballots counted yesterday, Proposition B had 105,336... 53,177 against' - conservative floor (top-contest sum)

observations.label: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL)
observations.citation: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL), 1995-12-13 (0EB4F70FC8AEDEDE): MID-NIGHT partial, 339 of 551 precincts: Brown 73,403 / Jordan 61,828 - conservative floor; true night-end higher (power-era punch-card delays)

observations.label: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL)
observations.citation: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL), 1995-11-10 (0EB4F6FF665242DA): results table 'totals as of yesterday, with some absentee ballots still to be counted', 543/543 precincts, mayor sum 195,267 - conservative floor

observations.label: SF Chronicle archive
observations.citation: SF Chronicle 1995-11-10 'registrar operating under a cloud' (sfchronicle.com/news/article/3121516): 'roughly 10,000 absentee ballots left to count' - conservative floor, certified minus stated remaining

observations.citation: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL), 'S.F. Voters Pass Bond Measure For Art Museum' 1994-11-10 pB5: 'registrar officials said about 10,000 absentee ballots were still uncounted'; companion piece itemizes 6,000 absentee + 4,000 provisional - conservative floor

observations.label: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL)
observations.citation: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL), 1994-06-09 (0EB4F5EC914918D5): night table 551/551 precincts, Prop A 76,181 yes / 34,793 no - conservative night floor (top-contest sum)

observations.label: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL)
observations.citation: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL), 1993-06-16 (0EB4F5238AE9867C): night count Prop A 58,495 yes / 20,843 no - conservative night floor (top-contest sum)

observations.label: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL)
observations.citation: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL), 1991-12-11 (13E41D5657CC3578): night table 627/627 precincts, Jordan 98,491 / Agnos 90,875 - conservative night floor (mayor-contest sum)

observations.label: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL)
observations.citation: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL), 1991-12-18 (13E41D5271A05880): 'The official tally released yesterday... include[s] contested ballots not counted on election day' - count complete day 7

observations.label: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL)
observations.citation: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL), 1991-11-06 (13E41D6D56423A18): night table 628/628 precincts, mayor contest sum 187,990 (Jordan 59,928, Agnos 51,714, Alioto 34,910, Hsieh 18,241, Hongisto 17,663 + minor) - conservative night floor

observations.label: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL)
observations.citation: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL), 1990-11-09 (0EB4F2D1A68C546B): headline '2,000 ballots remain to be examined in San Francisco' - conservative floor

observations.label: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL)
observations.citation: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL), 1990-06-09 (0EB4F2776A49A703): citywide judge race 'about 1,000 left to count' by late June 8 - conservative floor

observations.label: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL)
observations.citation: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL), 1989-11-08 (0EB4F1E1F451DBA3): night results table 621/621 precincts, Prop P China Basin Ballpark 84,618 yes / 86,592 no (the measure that lost by 1,974) - top-contest sum is a conservative night floor

observations.label: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL)
observations.citation: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL), 1988-11-10 (0EB4F0EB158C3BF3): election-night state 'with most votes counted, except about 40,000 absentee ballots' - conservative night floor

observations.label: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL)
observations.citation: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL), 1988-11-10 (0EB4F0EB2983145D): figures 'announced late yesterday afternoon... did not include about 2,500 absentee ballots still to be counted' - conservative floor; 40,000 absentees counted in one day

observations.label: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL)
observations.citation: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL), 1987-11-05 (0EB4EFBFBAB11E9B): 'Yesterday he [Registrar Patterson] reported that 188,850 votes were cast Tuesday' - registrar day-1 count

observations.label: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL)
observations.citation: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL), 1987-04-09 (docref 0EB4EF2D8E65D869): 'An analysis of the 108,563 votes cast Tuesday' - single-contest special, contest count ~= ballots

observations.citation: SF Chronicle via NewsBank (SFPL), 'Feinstein's Name Failed To Carry Her Big Issues' 1986-06-05 p1: 'Registrar of Voters Jay Patterson said about 4000 absentee ballots have yet to be counted' - conservative floor

observations.label: SF Chronicle page scan (NewsBank/SFPL)
observations.citation: SF Chronicle 1983-11-09 p24 'S.F. VOTE' table, 100% precincts reporting: Prop A (Recall Election) 106,762 yes / 35,947 no = 142,709 - conservative night floor (top-contest sum; PCT column 75/25 validates); NewsBank image scan mirror/newsbank/scans/19831108_issue19831109_p23.png via SFPL, read 2026-06-11

observations.label: SF Chronicle page scan (NewsBank/SFPL)
observations.citation: SF Chronicle (NewsBank image scan via SFPL), 1982-11-03 p4 'Nelder Leads the Pack in S.F. Supervisors Race': 'With all the votes cast yesterday counted...' / 'The race probably will not be decided until 8,000 absentee ballots, turned in at polling places yesterday, are counted later this week' - conservative floor, certified minus stated remaining; scan ...19821102_issue19821103_p4.png, verified 2026-06-11

observations.label: SF Chronicle page scan (NewsBank/SFPL)
